message:
fix(fcp-datadog): enforce Datadog endpoint host allow-list at configure boundary (resolves br-ieiby)

Resolves flywheel_connectors-ieiby — [REVIEW-A9][P1] connector
security: configure() previously accepted any base_url verbatim,
so api_key + app_key bearer pairs could be routed to attacker-
controlled hosts.

**Previous behavior (auth-routing-to-attacker):**

`DatadogConfig::from_params` accepted any string as `base_url`,
constructed a `DatadogClient` (carrying the credential pair) at
connector.rs:180 BEFORE any endpoint check ran. The
`provisioning_readiness` view did a SUBSTRING diagnostic
(`base_url.contains("datadoghq.com")`), which:

1. Ran AFTER the client was already wired with creds, so was
   informational only.
2. Was substring-trivial: `https://datadoghq.com.evil.example/api/v1`
   passes `.contains("datadoghq.com")` and would have been reported
   as `network_ok=true` despite being attacker-controlled.

**Fix (mirrors zapier 63493e6e and teams 9a2aabd2):**

- New `validate_base_url` helper enforces:
  - https scheme required (or localhost/127.0.0.1 for tests).
  - Exact host equality against the
    `ALLOWED_DATADOG_HOSTS` allow-list:
    `api.datadoghq.com`, `api.us3.datadoghq.com`,
    `api.us5.datadoghq.com`, `api.ap1.datadoghq.com`,
    `api.datadoghq.eu`. Mirrors the
    `DatadogRegion::api_base_url` set in `client.rs`.
- `DatadogConfig::from_params` calls `validate_base_url(&base_url)?`
  before returning. Off-policy → `FcpError::InvalidRequest { code:
  1003 }`. The `DatadogClient` is never constructed for an off-
  policy URL.
- Uses `reqwest::Url::parse` for host extraction (already a
  transitive dep via `reqwest`); no new direct dependency.

**Regression tests (5 new + 1 inverted, all passing):**

- `config_base_url_overrides_region_within_policy` (was
  `_overrides_region`): inverted from asserting
  `https://custom.example.com/api/v1` is accepted (the bug) to
  asserting `https://api.us3.datadoghq.com/api/v1` is accepted
  (the policy-conforming path).
- `config_rejects_non_datadog_host`: `https://custom.example.com`
  → InvalidRequest naming the policy.
- `config_rejects_substring_collision_host`: locks in exact-host
  equality vs. the legacy substring check —
  `datadoghq.com.evil.example` AND `evil-datadoghq.com` both
  rejected.
- `config_rejects_http_non_local_host`: `http://api.datadoghq.com`
  rejected (https required).
- `config_accepts_localhost_for_tests`: `localhost` and
  `127.0.0.1` paths preserved for tests.
- `config_accepts_every_region_default_url`: regression guard —
  if a future region adds an api_base_url whose host is not in
  ALLOWED_DATADOG_HOSTS, this fails fast.

272 fcp-datadog tests passing (226 lib + 2 doctests + 44
integration). Same defense pattern as br-898o4 (zapier base_url)
and the corresponding teams fix: validate at construction, not
surface violations as informational status fields callers may
ignore.
